data.content:
**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes the file but never adds it to the `Compile` item group. Implicit globbing expands at evaluation time (before targets run), so the newly created file is invisible to the compiler.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to `<Compile>` and `<FileWrites>` inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add to Compile so the compiler sees it, and FileWrites so Clean removes it -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it never adds the generated file to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild's glob expansion happens at evaluation time (before any targets run), so the file doesn't exist yet when globs are expanded. The target needs to explicitly add the generated file to the `<Compile>` item group.

Let me fix this using the `including-generated-files` skill knowledge:
- Add the generated file to `<Compile Include="...">` in the target
- Also add it to `<FileWrites Include="...">` so it gets cleaned properly

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add to Compile so the compiler sees it, and FileWrites so Clean removes it -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds now. Here's the diagnosis:

**Problem:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target creates `GeneratedInfo.cs` during the build, but MSBuild's implicit `<Compile>` glob expands at **evaluation time** — before any targets run. Since the file doesn't exist yet during evaluation, the compiler never sees it.

**Fix:** Added an `<ItemGroup>` inside the target to explicitly include the generated file in `Compile` (so Roslyn compiles it) and `FileWrites` (so `dotnet clean` removes it).
